page: Amazon Web Services c5d.4xlarge is a compute-optimized instance built on the Nitro hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by an Intel Xeon 8124M processor operating at 3.4 GHz. The configuration provides 16 dedicated vCPUs across 8 physical cores with hyperthreading enabled. Memory resources include 32.0 GB of DDR4 RAM running at 3 GHz, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB per core. Local storage is bundled, consisting of a 400 GB NVMe SSD instance store volume, and baseline network bandwidth is rated at 5 Gbps.

Benchmark evaluations place this instance primarily in the average performance tier. The stress-ng single-core CPU performance score is 1405.49, while the multi-core score reaches 14734.77. In memory bandwidth testing, the instance achieves 823.29 GB/sec for cached small-block reads, dropping to 80.5 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads and 38.76 GB/sec for large-block writes. Software compilation with Clang scores 12847.0 on Geekbench, and static web serving throughput is measured at 12.39 GB/sec.

Tradeoffs are evident in specific data decompression and language model tasks. The bzip3 decompression benchmark shows weak performance, registering 8.73 GB/sec single-threaded and 35.8 GB/sec multi-threaded. Similarly, LLM inference prompt processing for a medium 7B model is weak at 22.77 tokens/sec, though text generation for the same model averages 10.92 tokens/sec. Given the compute-heavy vCPU-to-RAM ratio and local NVMe storage, this instance is configured for workloads such as database operations, web serving, and software compilation where local scratch space is required.
description: Amazon Web Services c5d.4xlarge is an x86_64 compute-optimized instance featuring 16 dedicated vCPUs from an Intel Xeon 8124M processor running at 3.4 GHz. It is equipped with 32.0 GB of DDR4 RAM, a 400 GB local NVMe SSD instance store, and a 5 Gbps baseline network interface. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, the instance delivers average performance across most benchmarks, including a Geekbench multi-core score of 7156.0 and a static web serving throughput of 12.39 GB/sec. However, it exhibits weak performance in bzip3 decompression and medium-model LLM prompt processing. The qualitative cost efficiency of this hardware profile is defined by its dedicated compute resources and high-speed local storage, making it suitable for database operations, software compilation, and web serving workloads that require fast local scratch space.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c5d.4xlarge is an x86_64 compute-optimized instance with 16 vCPUs, 32 GB DDR4 RAM, and a 400 GB local NVMe SSD, delivering balanced compute and local storage performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c5d.4xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 16 vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and a 400 GB local NVMe SSD on the Nitro hypervisor.
tagline: A compute-optimized architecture featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors and integrated local NVMe SSD storage for high-throughput local scratch operations.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8124M processor with 16 dedicated vCPUs and 3.4 GHz clock speed
32.0 GB of DDR4 RAM providing 2.0 GB per core
400 GB of local NVMe SSD instance storage
5 Gbps baseline network bandwidth on the Nitro hypervisor
Suited for database operations, software compilation, and web serving
